content:
{
  "title": "Adversarially Contrastive Prompting: Dynamic Contextual Augmentation for Jailbreak Robustness in LLMs",
  "problem": "Current LLM prompting methods lack mechanisms to proactively adapt to adversarial or jailbreaking attempts within conversation, resulting in persistent vulnerabilities. We aim to design a prompting strategy that detects and dynamically counteracts adversarial prompt structures during inference.",
  "motivation": "LLMs are increasingly deployed in sensitive environments where jailbreaks can have significant societal impacts. Prior defenses have focused on static prompt engineering, finetuning, or adaptive shield prompts with fixed heuristics, but lack contextual adaptability to unseen adversarial triggers. A dynamic, context-sensitive prompting mechanism could generalize better against evolving attack strategies.",
  "method": "We propose Adversarially Contrastive Prompting (ACP), a prompting meta-method that injects lightweight contrastive sub-prompts into the user context at inference. These sub-prompts are generated dynamically in response to detected adversarial prompt features using a learned adversarial feature extractor. The sub-prompts explicitly contrast harmful completions by conditioning the LLM response away from predicted adversarial intents, without revealing the detection logic to the user. ACP operates at the prompt level, does not require additional model training, and can be composed with any pre-trained LLM.",
  "experiment_plan": "We will evaluate ACP on jailbreak and adversarial prompt datasets, including JailbreakBench and real-world red-teaming corpora. Baselines include AdaShield, static system prompts, and post-hoc content filtering. Key metrics are attack success rate, helpfulness, and task completion. Ablation studies will assess detection and contrastive sub-prompt efficacy separately."
}
